goal: Internationalize the app to 6 locales (i18n framework + per-locale translation + locale routing)

tasks.title: Split the PR into two?
tasks._comment: awaiting-user node — carries a decision_package (agent-shaped soft edge, hooks ignore it; board-lint R8a requires it on blocked_on:"user" nodes so /cc-master:discuss can open the interview).

decision_package.context_md: The i18n rollout has grown into one large branch: the framework scaffold (T0, verified) plus the per-locale translation pass (T1, in flight) plus the locale-routing module (M1). That is three logically separable concerns on one branch. Before we open the review, I need to know whether to ship it as a single PR or split it. Splitting changes how I sequence the remaining work (M1.c still has an open dep on T1), so this is a routing decision only you should make — I won't pick the PR boundary on my own.
decision_package.question: Ship the i18n rollout as one PR, or split it into framework+translation vs locale-routing?
decision_package.what_i_need: Pick one of the options below (or name a different split). Once you decide, I'll set the branch/PR boundaries accordingly and re-sequence M1.c against T1.
decision_package.why_it_matters: Every remaining work node funnels into the eventual PR. A split changes the merge order and the review surface; deciding late means re-cutting branches after work has already piled onto one. This node gates the wrap-up — the leaves can finish, but I can't open review until the boundary is fixed.

options.label: One PR — framework + translation + routing together
options.rationale: All three concerns are part of the same feature flag and ship together; a single PR keeps the i18n story reviewable as one coherent change and avoids cross-PR dep choreography.
options.tradeoffs: ✅ One review, one merge, no inter-PR ordering. ⚠️ Large diff (framework + 6 locales + routing) is heavier to review; a regression in one concern blocks the whole merge.

options.label: Two PRs — (1) framework + translation, (2) locale routing
options.rationale: Framework+translation is independently shippable and verifiable; locale-routing (M1) can land second once T1 is merged, matching M1.c's existing dep on T1.
options.tradeoffs: ✅ Smaller, focused reviews; routing can iterate without re-reviewing translations. ⚠️ Two merges to sequence (PR 2 rebases on PR 1); M1.c stays blocked until PR 1 lands.

tasks.title: Legacy task (pre-time-schema board) — old aliases still render
Module: locale routing (owner of a 3-task sub-DAG)
tasks._comment: owner = container node; its children carry parent:"M1". depth=1 type-invariant: a node with a parent cannot itself be a parent.

tasks.title: Per-locale route binding
tasks._comment: deps points within the same owner sub-graph (M1.a).

tasks.title: Wire routing into translated bundles
tasks._comment: open deps: a child of M1 depends on a non-M1 top-level node (T1) — scheduling edge (deps) and container edge (parent) are orthogonal.

log.summary: Opened locale-routing module as an owner node with a 3-task nested sub-DAG (M1.a/M1.b/M1.c via parent:"M1")
Surfaced PR-split question to user as D1 (async user dependency)
